bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am opening with a high-utility bid targeting my maximum possible utility. Since there is no evidence about the opponent’s priorities, I should start by anchoring on my top value for every issue and only concede later if their bids reveal tradeoffs.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still the very start of the negotiation (t=0.02), so there is little reason to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s first counteroffer gives me low utility and their message is aggressive cheap talk rather than evidence of real constraints, but I will make a small concession on a lower-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my opening bid.

It is still very early (t=0.04), so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a modest but visible concession on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers are actually not improving for me, and their aggressive messages are cheap talk, so I will not make a large concession yet; I’ll hold firm on high-weight issueE, issueB, and issueD while moving on issueA/issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 because it is very early at t=0.06 and the opponent’s actual offers are not improving for me despite their complaints. Their messages demand larger movement, but their bids remain anchored near my low-utility values, so I am making a meaningful but low-cost concession mainly on lower-weight issues while preserving issueE and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.75 because it is very early (t=0.08) and the opponent has not actually conceded in their bids, despite aggressive messages. Their offers repeatedly signal interest in issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueA, and issueE=valueA, so I am conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueC and making a moderate concession on issueB while holding firm on my most important issueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility because it is very early (t=0.10), but I am making a visible concession from my last bid by moving issueD to the opponent’s latest value while holding firmer on high-importance issueE. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and their messages are mostly pressure tactics, so I will not over-concede yet.

I am targeting a still-strong utility while making a further early-stage concession, since t=0.12 is too early to give up my most important issueE. The opponent’s offers have stayed very low for me and their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their bids suggest they prefer issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, and lower issueE; I will concede a small amount on issueB while holding firm on issueE.

I am targeting a moderate utility while it is still early (t=0.14), so I should not concede my highest-weight issueE yet. The opponent’s actual offers have consistently demanded issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB, and recently issueD=valueC, so I am matching those signals while holding firm on issueE, which matters most to me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.55 while making a visible concession on the main disputed issue, since it is still early at t=0.16 and there is no need to drop near the opponent’s low-utility offers. The opponent’s bids have only modestly improved for me and their messages focus heavily on issueE, so I will concede issueE one step while holding the already-conceded positions on the other issues.

I targeted a utility around 0.46, making a meaningful concession on issueE because the opponent’s messages and bids strongly signal that issueE is important to them, while still keeping the bid above the best utility their offers have provided me so far. Since time is still early at t=0.18, I am not matching their very low-utility counter, but I am avoiding a repeat of my prior bid and moving on the issue they keep pressing hardest.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.52: still above the opponent’s latest offer to me, but with substantial concessions on low/medium-priority issues. Since time is only at 0.20, I do not need to concede my highest-weight issueE all the way to their preferred value yet; however, their history shows they consistently want issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueB and have recently offered issueD=valueE, so I align on those while holding issueE at valueC.

I am targeting a utility a little above 0.50: we are still early at t=0.22, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s latest low-utility bid, but I can make a visible concession from my last offer. The opponent’s actual offers show they are firm on issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, and issueC=valueB, while their messages emphasize issueD/issueE; I will concede slightly on issueD while holding issueE at valueC, which they have already offered.

I am targeting a utility a little above 0.50: I have already conceded the low-weight issues A/C and the moderately important issueB to the opponent’s repeated preferred positions, but at t=0.24 it is still too early to drop issueE below valueC. The opponent’s offers and messages show they are pressing hardest on issueE while holding issueA/issueB/issueC mostly fixed; I will therefore keep issueE at valueC and use issueD: valueE, which they have previously proposed, as a potentially acceptable tradeoff.

I am targeting a utility just above 0.51: still protective of my highest-weight issueE by keeping it at valueC, while making a small concession on issueD to match the opponent’s latest valid position. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-utility bids; their messages are aggressive but their actual offers show firmness on issueA/issueB/issueC and some movement on issueD/issueE.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.45, which is a meaningful concession from my recent offers while still preserving value on issueE, my most important issue. At t=0.28 there is time left, but the opponent has repeatedly held low positions and specifically pushed on issueE, so I will match most of their latest structure while not conceding issueE all the way down.

At t=0.30 there is still enough time to avoid fully capitulating, but the opponent’s offers have gradually moved into a range that is acceptable to work from. I am preserving value on the most important issueE by keeping valueC, while accepting their latest positions on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD to make a meaningful concession without dropping too close to reservation.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.48, which is a meaningful concession from my last offer while still well above reservation value. At t=0.32 there is time left, but the opponent has been fairly stubborn; their messages are mostly pressure tactics, while their actual offers show they may accept issueA=valueA, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueB, issueD=valueC, issueE=valueB because they offered exactly that recently.
